stories.headline: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
stories.summary: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.

stories.headline: Iran rejects ceasefire proposal with US as war of words deepens
stories.summary: Iran rejects a new 45-day ceasefire proposal, while the US is still expected to respond, as US President Donald Trump delays a threatened military strike tied to the Strait of Hormuz. Mediators are pushing for talks, but tensions remain high on both sides.

stories.headline: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.summary: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.headline: Israeli army fire on WHO vehicle in southern Gaza kills one, medics report
stories.summary: WHO driver Majdi Aslan was killed and a WHO doctor wounded, along with several other Palestinians.

stories.headline: Jobless young Palestinians trapped as Israel holds Gaza’s economy hostage
stories.summary: With 80 percent unemployed, young Palestinians struggle to survive as Israeli blockade causes economic collapse

stories.headline: Emerging Assets Pare Gains as Iran Rebuffs Ceasefire Proposal
stories.summary: Emerging-market assets trimmed gains after a report that Iran has rebuffed a ceasefire proposal dented hopes for a quick resolution to the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes Russian Black Sea energy hub Novorossiysk
stories.summary: Ukraine increases attacks on Russia's energy infrastructure in bid to disrupt financing of its war.

stories.headline: How US operation to rescue air officer from Iran unfolded
stories.summary: President Trump says US service member whose fighter jet was shot down over Iran is rescued during two raids.

stories.headline: EU warns capitals against turning energy crunch into fiscal crisis
stories.summary: Excessive spending to cushion soaring prices would have ‘serious fiscal implications’, says economy commissioner

stories.headline: Ukraine Says Drones Set Russian Black Sea Oil Terminal on Fire
stories.summary: Russia’s key Black Sea oil terminal caught fire following an overnight drone attack, Ukraine’s General Staff said on Monday, confirming earlier satellite images from NASA.

stories.headline: A Harrowing Race Against Time to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
stories.summary: For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.

stories.headline: U.S. Rescues Missing Air Force Officer Whose Fighter Jet Was Downed by Iran
stories.summary: An Air Force officer of a F-15E Strike Eagle shot down on Friday by Iran spent a day in hostile territory with little more than a pistol for protection.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security in unexpected military alliance
stories.summary: Ukraine's Zelenskyy arrived in Damascus in a surprise visit on Sunday, for his first official visit to Syria after the fall of the pro-Russian al-Assad regime.

stories.headline: Track current petrol and diesel prices, service station outages and shipments – Australia's fuel crisis in charts
stories.summary: How much fuel does Australia have left today, and when could we run out? Check how much petrol and diesel prices have risen near you in Sydney, Melbourne and across the country since the US and Israel’s war on Iran began in late FebruaryGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastHundreds of service stations across Australia have run empty, fuel prices are elevated and oil shipments have been cancelled.Australia is battling a fuel

stories.headline: Trio of Ghost Ships Left in Venezuela Limbo After Maduro’s Exit
stories.summary: Three dark fleet tankers that loaded Venezuelan oil before the ousting of leader Nicolás Maduro remain stranded in the country, underscoring how its oil industry is still disentangling itself from years of sanctions evasion.

stories.headline: Earnings a 'Blessing' for Markets, Citi's Kaiser Says
stories.summary: Stuart Kaiser, Citi head of US equity trading strategy, says earnings growth expectations are helping to offset the increasing investor concerns about the war in Iran. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their nameAt the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common.Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Southeast Asia oil crisis puts sovereign ratings at risk
Indonesia raises fuel surcharges as oil crisis sparks airline industry outcry
Mitsotakis urges swift EU probe into MPs over Greek farm subsidy fraud
stories.summary: Greek PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis has asked EU prosecutors to swiftly charge MPs over alleged €23m farm subsidy fraud, as resignations and pressure mount.

stories.headline: Why this oil shock is different
stories.summary: Governments and central banks are out of policy ammunition to contain the economic fallout

stories.headline: Sales of used EVs surge in US as petrol prices pass $4 a gallon
stories.summary: Americans are buying second-hand electric vehicles even as the market for new vehicles slumps

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia charges record premium for its oil
stories.summary: World’s biggest crude exporter to ask Asian customers for around $20 a barrel on top of benchmark prices

stories.headline: Leftist Economist Mello Gets Planning Job, Not Central Bank Post
stories.summary: Brazil’s new Planning Minister Bruno Moretti will name leftist economist Guilherme Mello as his deputy, removing him from contention for a seat on the central bank’s board and advancing a shakeup of the federal government’s economic team ahead of elections, according to a person familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Growth Slows at US Service Providers as Price Gauge Surges
stories.summary: The US service economy expanded in March at a slower pace as employment shrank by the most since 2023 and input prices accelerated sharply.

stories.headline: Cotton Climbs to Highest Since 2024 on Tight Supply Outlook
stories.summary: Cotton futures in New York surged to the highest since December 2024 as traders weigh a preliminary government US planting report against an outlook for tighter global supplies this year.

stories.headline: Industrial Firm Madison Air Seeks $2.23 Billion in IPO
stories.summary: Madison Air Solutions Corp. is seeking to raise as much as $2.23 billion in its initial public offering, in what would be the biggest US listing of an industrial company in close to three decades.

stories.headline: Goldman Traders See ‘Fast-Money’ Funds Turning Into Stock Buyers
stories.summary: Systematic investors are poised to flip back into equity-buying mode after slashing their exposure to multi-year lows during the recent market selloff, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s trading desk.

stories.headline: Wall Street Banks Line Up Financing for €1.5 Billion Sushi Deal
stories.summary: Wall Street banks are lending €750 million ($867 million) to finance the roughly €1.5 billion tie-up of Asian food producer Eat Happy Group and sushi supplier Hana Group SAS’ European operations, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Barings Caps Redemptions as Private Credit Holders Seek 11%
stories.summary: A $4.9 billion Barings LLC private credit fund capped redemptions after investors asked to pull out 11.3% of the shares in the first quarter.

stories.headline: The California Lake Billed as the ‘Saudi Arabia of Lithium’
stories.summary: Residents of Imperial County, Calif., are in dire need of an economic boost. Experts say the answer lies beneath the Salton Sea, where a lithium trove sits.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Dismissal of Bannon Conviction
stories.summary: Stephen K. Bannon, a former close aide to President Trump, was convicted for failing to comply with a congressional subpoena related to the investigation into the Jan. 6 attack.

stories.headline: Ahead of 2026 Midterms, Republicans Unveil a $342 Million Battle Plan to Keep the Senate
stories.summary: The main super PAC for Senate Republicans is focusing on eight states, and plans to spend big money to defend G.O.P.-held seats in Alaska, Iowa and Ohio.

stories.headline: Plunging International Student Enrollment Under Trump Squeezes Colleges
stories.summary: The Trump administration’s campaign to curtail international students is not just hitting the elite schools targeted by the government.

stories.headline: Diesel remains volatile as prices rise again despite Labor’s fuel tax relief
stories.summary: Energy minister Chris Bowen says 3.4% of Australia’s service stations had no diesel, as of Monday, after wholesale prices surgedTrack Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in chartsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastDiesel users in Australia are not enjoying the same relief as unleaded customers, with one in 30 service stations still entirely out of diesel and prices rising again after an initial s

stories.headline: Dozens of firms risk losing B Corp status after standards overhaul
stories.summary: Tougher ethical certification process requires companies to meet standards in every one out of seven categoriesDozens of companies may be at risk of losing their coveted B Corp ethical status after the organisation behind the corporate kite-marking system raised the standards required to qualify.B Lab, which oversees B Corp certification, launched the biggest overhaul in its 19-year history earlier this month, scrapping a system under which compa

stories.headline: Mazda halts production for Middle East exports through May
Malaysia launches push to encourage local investors to buy stocks
Japan's corporate bond issuance hits record high on retail investors
Why are we taxed to fly? Europe’s aviation levies explained
stories.summary: Airfare pricing is no simple business. From dynamic ticketing, to government policies and shifting global factors, what passengers end up paying can be highly unpredictable.

stories.headline: Foreign investment in Spain falls by 22% to lowest level since 2021
stories.summary: FDI in Spain fell by 21.8% in 2025 to €30.764 billion, its lowest level since 2021 despite the expected boost from European funds, according to official data.

stories.headline: Pro-Orbán actors accuse Meta of interference in Hungarian elections, despite lack of evidence
stories.summary: Meta has found itself embroiled in Hungary's tense election campaign, after supporters of the ruling Fidesz party claimed their posts are being restricted to favour opposition parties. In reality, there is no weight to the allegations.

stories.headline: Revellers parade giant phalluses at Japan’s fertility festival
stories.summary: Crowds pack Kawasaki for Kanamara Matsuri, Japan’s colourful fertility festival using phallic shrines to celebrate life and challenge sex taboos.

stories.headline: Oil Swings as Iran Rejects Ceasefire, Trump Reups Hormuz Demand
stories.summary: Oil swung after Iran rejected a proposed ceasefire and US President Donald Trump again threatened to bomb the country’s infrastructure if Tehran doesn’t cede to demands and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Trump threatens Iran with ‘Hell’ over Strait of Hormuz in profane post
stories.summary: Trump escalated threats against Iran’s power plants, bridges and other infrastructure in an expletive-laden post on Truth Social on Easter morning.

stories.headline: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
stories.summary: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.

stories.headline: Stocks Edge Up as Traders Weigh Ceasefire Chances: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Signs of last-ditch efforts to secure a truce in the war that has rattled global markets spurred a cautious advance in stocks as oil whipsawed.

stories.headline: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat
stories.summary: Countries around the world, including the US, have already stopped imports over the outbreak.

stories.headline: Cambodia unveils statue to honour famous landmine-sniffing rat
stories.summary: The statue honours the late African giant pouched rat who sniffed out more than 100 landmines during his lifetime.

stories.headline: Two protests, two elections: How Nepal's Gen Z succeeded where Bangladesh's stumbled
stories.summary: How did a youth movement triumph in one country but fall so short in another?

stories.headline: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
stories.summary: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

stories.headline: Why McDonald’s and KFC are growing like wildfire in China
stories.summary: Western fast-food brands are expanding into the countryside

stories.headline: High Gas Prices Push More Drivers to Consider EVs
stories.summary: From the gas pump to the auto show, drivers are talking about electric vehicles.

stories.headline: Mexican art world protests over plan to send Frida Kahlo masterpieces to Spain
stories.summary: Cultural figures sign open letter asking government for clarity on how long landmark collection will remain abroadOne of the world’s most important collections of 20th-century Mexican art, including works by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera, is set to be exported to Spain under an agreement with Banco Santander, sparking outrage among Mexico’s cultural community.Nearly 400 cultural professionals have signed an open letter calling on the Mexican gover

stories.summary: Exclusive: Animal welfare charities ‘bitterly disappointed’ UK government plans to backtrack on manifesto promisesThis article contains an image of a duck being force-fed that some readers may find upsettingThe UK government is to break a manifesto commitment to ban foie gras imports, and has declined to stop fur imports, after the EU made these red lines in its discussions for a trade deal.Animal welfare charities say they are “bitterly disappoi

stories.headline: What we know so far about the search for missing US airman in Iran
stories.summary: US and Iranian forces are searching for a missing American crew member after a US warplane was shot down.

stories.headline: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
stories.summary: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

stories.headline: Trump files emergency appeal to keep building White House ballroom
stories.summary: “Time is of the essence!” Justice Department lawyers wrote, saying a ruling to pause construction puts the president at risk.

stories.headline: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
stories.summary: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

stories.headline: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
stories.summary: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

stories.headline: A day in the life of Asia’s fuel crisis
stories.summary: From farms in New Zealand to factories in Delhi, the effects of the oil crisis triggered by the Iran war are rippling across Asia Continue reading...

stories.headline: Watch: Who is the coup leader who will be Myanmar's next president?
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing presided over his last military parade in Myanmar before he takes up the role of president.

stories.headline: Italy's Uffizi Galleries targeted in cyber-attack but deny security breach
stories.summary: Hackers were reported to have infiltrated IT systems - but the gallery says its works are safe.

stories.headline: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warningOne person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves.The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
stories.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
stories.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

stories.headline: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".

stories.headline: Alleged Bondi gunman loses court bid to suppress names of his family
stories.summary: Lawyers for Naveed Akram had argued his mother, brother and sister live in "constant fear".

stories.headline: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

stories.headline: Asia's migrant workers debate if Gulf jobs are worth deadly risk of Iran war
stories.summary: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

stories.headline: Huge fires at Russian oil facilities following Ukraine strikes, satellite images show
stories.summary: BBC Verify examines recent damage to three key Russian oil export facilities near the Baltic Sea.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gustsAn unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia.
